Refactor schedule feature: service, models, UI states (#157)

* Refactor schedule feature: service, models, UI states

Major architectural overhaul of schedule feature:
- Centralized all schedule data transformation in ScheduleService
- Added apiSitesSiteIdScheduleGet endpoint and facade wrapper
- Extracted shared models (DaySchedule, TimeSlotUI, SlotDisplayState) to individual files with barrel export
- Refactored ScheduleComponent to manage state only; WeeklyPlanner and ScheduleTable are now pure presentation components
- Removed legacy types and transformation logic from components
- Updated BookState enum (Plaid → Played) and UI to support all booking states
- Introduced SlotDisplayState for frontend slot state mapping and CSS class binding
- Improved table/button styling and added "Played" state support
- Added detailed documentation on architecture and best practices
- Verified build; no breaking changes to public API

Benefits: improved maintainability, testability, type safety, and separation of concerns.

commit.message:
Refactor code structure for improved readability and data handling (#35)

* Refactor code structure for improved readability and maintainability

* Refactor data fetching: introduce fetchBundle method and mapBundleToMmmPayload for improved payload handling

* Update version to 0.7.3 in package.json and package-lock.json

* fix/refactor: remove duplicates, fix bugs, clarify naming

Bugs fixed:
- absences.js: nowYmd now respects ctx._currentTodayYmd so debugDate
  works correctly (was always using new Date() instead)
- dataFetchOrchestrator.js: fix 3 activeLogger calls with wrong
  signature (1 arg → text was passed as level, not message)

Renamed for clarity (no behaviour change):
- formatDateYYYYMMDD → formatDateISO in dateTimeUtils.js
  (was misleadingly named; returns YYYY-MM-DD, not YYYYMMDD)
- normalizeTimeToMinutes → normalizeTimeToHHMM in dataOrchestration.js
  and webuntisClient.js (returns HHMM integer, not minutes)
- sanitizeHtmlText → stripAllHtml in dataOrchestration.js
  (distinguishes it from sanitizeHtml which keeps a whitelist of tags)
- initWidget → resolveWidgetHelpers in util.js and all 6 widget files
  (function resolves helpers, does not initialise anything;
  initWidget kept as backward-compat alias)
- getFirstChangedName → getFirstFieldName in grid.js
  ("Changed" was misleading; returns current name of first array entry)
- studentCell → studentLabelText in absences/exams/homework/lessons.js
  (variable holds a string, not a DOM element)
- _fetchAppData: replace hidden arguments[3] with explicit options = {}
  parameter in authService.js

Duplicates removed:
- isIrregularStatus, getChangedFieldSet, getFirstFieldName were defined
  identically in both grid.js and lessons.js; moved to util.js and
  exported via resolveWidgetHelpers
- tryOrNull in errorUtils.js now delegates to tryOrDefault(fn, null)
  instead of duplicating the try/catch body
- Removed unused formatHolidayDate alias from util.js

Docs updated: ARCHITECTURE.md reflects renamed functions

* refactor: centralize parent authentication flow in _getParentAuthResult method

* refactor: rename time-related utility functions for clarity and introduce new parsing function

* refactor: extract common context retrieval into _extractRestRequestContext and simplify validation warnings collection

* refactor: streamline REST endpoint execution and authentication handling in WebUntisClient

* refactor: update time-related utility functions and remove legacy compatibility code

* refactor: introduce buildFetchFlags method and streamline widget fetch logic in WebUntisClient and node_helper

* refactor: streamline fetch logic by consolidating parameters and enhancing context handling in WebUntisClient and related modules

* refactor: enhance date range calculation by adopting a flat input structure and improving parameter handling in calculateFetchRanges

* refactor: enhance fetch logic by restructuring context handling and introducing a generic input shape for date range calculations

* refactor: enhance data fetching logic by introducing reusable fetch functions and improving response handling in WebUntis API service

* feat: Implement mmmPayloadMapper and payloadBuilder for structured data handling

- Added mmmPayloadMapper.js to map data bundles to MMM payloads.
- Introduced payloadBuilder.js to construct GOT_DATA payloads with compacting and warning management.
- Enhanced authService.js to improve logger injection for better flexibility.
- Updated dataFetchOrchestrator.js to include homework filter parameters for fetching homework data.
- Modified webuntisClient.js to integrate new payload mapping and homework filter logic.
- Adjusted node_helper.js to pass homework filter settings from student configuration.

* refactor: update architecture documentation to reflect timetable-first orchestration and improved fetch strategy

* refactor: update architecture documentation to reflect changes in function line numbers and service orchestration

* Fix holiday key mismatch, allEnd null guard, fetchFromTargets early-exit, and dead code removal (#36)

* Initial plan

* fix: address review comments - holiday key consistency, allEnd default, fetchFromTargets sentinel, remove dead code

Wire up ICodeflow on ProductConstructionServiceApi (#6057)

The `Codeflow` generated client class and `ICodeflow` interface were
added in #6034 but were not wired up on `IProductConstructionServiceApi`
or `ProductConstructionServiceApi`, making the new codeflow statuses
endpoint inaccessible through the typed client.

This adds:
- `ICodeflow Codeflow { get; }` to `IProductConstructionServiceApi`
- `public ICodeflow Codeflow { get; }` property to
`ProductConstructionServiceApi`
- `Codeflow = new Codeflow(this);` in the constructor

4 lines, mirrors the pattern used by all other service operations
(Assets, Builds, Subscriptions, etc.).

commit.message:
feat: add nested icon support for buttons

- Button converter now handles nested icon children
- Nested icons are converted to empty wp:etch/svg blocks
- Non-icon children are safely ignored (only icons supported)
- Added 4 comprehensive tests for icon-in-button scenarios:
  * Single nested icon
  * Multiple nested icons
  * Mixed children (icons + other elements)
  * Style inheritance for nested icons
- All 201 unit tests now passing (100%)

ormalize_path\ properties, \module_key\ determinism

- \q(x,x)\ passes, \
e(x,x)\ fails, \gt(x,x)\ fails, \gte(x,x)\ passes

- Exists on present/absent keys, pointer root resolution
- Ratchet identity and large-increase invariants

### tokmd-badge (13 tests)

- Width positive, height == 24, label/value presence

test(w72): mutation-hardening tests for math + redact + path (~45 tests) (#603)

## Summary

Add mutation-hardening integration tests targeting common
mutation-testing survivor patterns across three crates.

### Test coverage

| Crate | Tests | Targets |
|-------|-------|---------|
| \ okmd-math\ | 20 | \ ound_f64\ boundaries, \safe_ratio\ div-by-zero,
\percentile\ edge cases, \gini_coefficient\ inequality ordering |
| \ okmd-redact\ | 15 | Hash length/determinism, separator
normalization, dot-segment cleaning, redact never returns original |
| \ okmd-path\ | 15 | Backslash conversion, empty strings, idempotency,
\../\ preservation, consecutive separators |

### Mutation patterns targeted
- **Boundary conditions**: exact .5 rounding, 0th/100th percentile,
empty slices
- **Boolean-logic flips**: \== 0\ guard in safe_ratio, \is_empty()\
checks
- **Comparison-operator swaps**: Gini monotonicity ordering, inequality
direction
- **Off-by-one errors**: percentile index computation, hash truncation
length
- **Constant replacement**: zero-return paths, identity ratios

commit.message:
fix(ci): scope CI mutants to fast core crates only (#219)

Algorithm crates (RSA, ECDSA, Ed25519, PGP, X509) and adapter crates
have slow key-generation tests that make mutation testing prohibitively
expensive in CI (RSA alone: 45 mutants x 365s timeout = 4.5 hours).

Introduce MUTANT_CRATES constant for CI, containing only core
microcrates and fast crates. Slow crates are still mutant-tested:
- When directly impacted in PR-scoped runs (cargo xtask pr)
- When manually invoked (cargo xtask mutants)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix platform color type in GenerateDifferenceMask and GenerateDifferenceImage

Use SKColorType.Bgra8888 explicitly for diff bitmaps, matching the
format used by GetNormalizedBitmap. The default SKImageInfo uses
PlatformColorType which is RGBA on macOS, causing channel swaps
when writing/reading SKColor values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Return diff images in platform color type, add encode roundtrip tests

GetPixelSpan<SKColor>() is a raw memory blit with no color-type
swizzle. SKColor's ARGB uint matches BGRA8888 byte layout on LE,
so raw span writes only produce correct pixels in BGRA8888 buffers.
On RGBA8888 platforms (e.g. macOS), writing SKColor to a platform-
default bitmap via raw span swaps R and B in the encoded output.

Fix: build diff in BGRA8888 for correct span writes, then canvas-
draw to a platform-default bitmap before returning. This gives
callers an image in the expected platform layout that encodes and
displays correctly on all platforms.

Add SKColorLayoutTest with encode-roundtrip tests that verify the
diff image channels survive PNG encode/decode regardless of platform.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
